Motion For Show Cause Hearing
Off Calendar Apr-20-2017 Continued To May-11-17 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 503
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Edward Mouton, Jr., self-represented litigant / plaintiff as successor-in-interest, appearing telephonically,
Kimberly Chu from Brayton Purcell, appearing as former counsel of record for plaintiff.

Ruling: Plaintiff's Motion for Show Cause Hearing.

The plaintiff's motion for show cause is called concurrently with the Trial Setting Conference (TSC) calendar. Appearances are stated in open court. Ms. Chu requests that the motion be heard as in camera proceeding due to any possible issues with regard to attorney client privilege, etc. The Court grants Ms. Chu's request and states that the matter will be called at the end of today's calendar. At the second call of the matter, the courtroom is cleared of all persons except Ms. Chu and court staff. Mr. Mouton remains present by a telephonic appearance. The Court addresses the TSC, discussions regarding Mr. Mouton securing an attorney and his release date in November are held. At the conclusion of discussions, the court continues the TSC to December 21, 2017 at 1:30 pm in Department 206. A date which falls after Mr. Moutons release. The Court orders Mr. Mouton or his attorney, if retained, present on 12/21/17 at 1:30 pm in Department 206 at 400 McAllister Street, San Francisco, CA.

The in camera proceeding with regard to the plaintiff's motion for show cause are held. At the conclusion of the in camera proceeding, the Court continued Plaintiff's Motion for Show Cause Hearing to May 11, 2017 at 2:00 pm in Department 206 and granted a telephonic appearance by Mr. Mouton on that date. Mr. Mouton confirms his address in open court. The clerk addresses Mr. Mouton's attempts at filing his change of address form with the court noting that the form has been rejected three times. Discussions regarding Mr. Mouton serving the change of address form were held. At the conclusion of the discussions, Mr. Mouton was directed to file a proper change of address form with the Court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerks: Jacqueline Alameda/J. Veluz, Matter Not Reported. (206)
